behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic (template text inspection against leakage rules). The behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow. Integration was considered but rejected because no cross-module handoff is required to expose this specific failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function on a template fixture that includes phase instructions copied from SKILL.md and assert it returns a violation; verify a clean template passes.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries in prompt assembly (template + interpolation + fixtures + final render). Unit is too low because leakage can be introduced during wiring. Higher levels were rejected: behavior text focuses on assembled prompt content, not real OS/network/subprocess or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the in-process harness prompt assembly path with realistic fixtures and interpolation, then assert final prompt text has no phase-instruction leakage patterns.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module/file-loading behavior: invocation + skill file loader + prompt/test wiring. Unit is too low because handoff/source-of-truth can fail only when modules interact. Agentic was considered but rejected because this behavior text does not require live-model judgment; it can be validated as context-source plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real invocation path with plugin/skill files present, and assert the test behavior is driven by loaded skill context (not hard-coded prompt directives), e.g., by proving behavior changes when file-sourced context changes.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is harness orchestration across configuration/file-variant loading while keeping prompt text constant. This is module interaction, not single-function logic. System rejected: no real network/subprocess/OS-specific primitive required beyond normal local file wiring. Agentic rejected because behavior is ability-to-run across variants, not model-output correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run the harness twice with identical user prompt text: once with baseline SKILL.md and once with degraded/missing variant; assert only skill-context source changes and execution still proceeds.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior changing when skill guidance regresses; a stubbed model could hide this and still pass. Integration was considered but rejected because the behavior explicitly requires a behavioral failure under identical prompt text, which is about model decision quality from context.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model test with baseline skill context and with degraded/removed section, using identical user prompt; assert degraded variant fails behavioral criteria while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is ordering and data handoff between model-response capture, artifact writer, and parser modules. Unit can miss sequencing bugs across components. Agentic rejected because assertion is about capture order, not model-output quality; System rejected because no real external infra is required beyond local module wiring/files.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke response-processing pipeline with a controlled model output and assert raw artifact is written first and contains unmodified output before parser/extractor execution.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is assertion logic itself (structure/schema validation semantics). This can be caught with direct fixtures to assertion functions/objects. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text is about what assertions enforce, not harness/module orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Feed assertion logic fixtures where keywords exist but schema path/role-field placement is wrong; assert failure. Feed structurally correct fixture; assert pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says "Under live model execution" and relies on real model outputs where structural mistakes can occur despite keyword presence. Mocked responses can hide true regression patterns. Workflow rejected because this behavior can be shown in a single model-response step, not necessarily multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model evaluations on prompts known to produce keyword-present but structurally invalid outputs after a regression; assert structural checks fail while keyword-only checks would pass.
